description: §49.9 — a bare `break` in a position not enclosed by any `while`, `do...while`, or `for...of` loop fires E-LOOP-001. This is the SPEC's own worked example (§49.9 E-LOOP-001 block). Complements the existing loop-005 (break crossing a function boundary) and loop-006 (duplicate labels): here the break is simply outside ALL loops. W-PROGRAM-001 co-fires incidentally (no `<program>` shell around the bare markup).
rationale: §49.9 E-LOOP-001: the compiler SHALL reject `break` that appears outside any enclosing loop. `break` may only appear inside a `while`, `do...while`, or `for...of` loop body.
